{"key_event":"1925 Constitution and Ibáñez’s authoritarian consolidation","reasoning":"Labor, student, and middle-class mobilization expanded markedly, but massacres, military intervention, censorship, and arrests left civil society less able to constrain the state by 1927 than in the preceding decade. Despite fiscal stress and parliamentary paralysis, the stronger presidency, Central Bank reforms, national police consolidation, and military-backed authoritarian rule raised overall state coercive and administrative capacity.","society_change":-0.6,"society_power":7.0,"state_change":0.6,"state_power":8.7}
